title: Philippine CPA Board Exam Review - Financial Accounting and Reporting (FAR)
description: Comprehensive review for the Philippine CPA Board Exam focusing on Financial Accounting and Reporting (FAR) section with over 200 practice questions, detailed explanations, and board exam simulations
shortDescription: Prepare for the FAR section of the Philippine CPA Board Exam with comprehensive lectures, practice problems, and board exam simulations
requirements: Access to accounting calculator or spreadsheet software
whatYouLearn: Master PFRS applications for financial statement preparation under Philippine setting
Analyze complex accounting transactions including business combinations, leases, and financial instruments
Prepare consolidated financial statements for parent-subsidiary relationships
Apply revenue recognition principles under PFRS 15
Account for property, plant and equipment, intangible assets, and investment property
Implement proper accounting for income taxes, employee benefits, and provisions
Prepare statement of cash flows using both direct and indirect methods
Navigate ethical considerations and professional responsibilities in financial reporting
Develop exam-taking strategies for the CPA Board Exam FAR section
Build confidence through timed practice exams and performance analytics
targetAudience: Accounting graduates preparing for the CPA Board Exam
Accountants seeking to strengthen PFRS knowledge
Finance professionals transitioning to accounting roles
Review center students seeking supplementary materials
Overseas Filipino Workers (OFWs) preparing for return to Philippine practice

sections.title: Section 1: Conceptual Framework and Philippine Financial Reporting Standards

lessons.title: Lesson 1: Conceptual Framework for Financial Reporting
lessons.description: Understanding the IASB Conceptual Framework and its application in Philippine setting under PFRS

lessons.title: Lesson 2: Philippine Financial Reporting Standards (PFRS) Framework
lessons.description: Overview of PFRS, their convergence with IFRS, and the standard-setting process in the Philippines

lessons.title: Lesson 3: Presentation of Financial Statements (PFRS 1/IAS 1)
lessons.description: Requirements for presenting financial statements including structure, content, and presentation guidelines

lessons.title: Lesson 4: Cash and Cash Equivalents
lessons.description: Accounting for cash, bank overdrafts, and cash equivalents including bank reconciliations

lessons.title: Lesson 5: Trade and Other Receivables
lessons.description: Accounting for receivables including valuation, impairment, and factoring arrangements

lessons.title: Lesson 6: Inventories
lessons.description: Accounting for inventories under PFRS 2/IAS 2 including cost formulas and net realizable value

lessons.title: Lesson 7: Biological Assets
lessons.description: Specialized accounting for living animals and plants under PFRS 41/IAS 41

lessons.title: Lesson 8: Property, Plant and Equipment
lessons.description: Accounting for PPE under PFRS 16/IAS 16 including recognition, measurement, and depreciation

lessons.title: Lesson 9: Investment Property
lessons.description: Accounting for property held to earn rentals or for capital appreciation under PFRS 40/IAS 40

lessons.title: Lesson 10: Goodwill and Intangible Assets
lessons.description: Accounting for intangible assets and goodwill under PFRS 38/IAS 38 and PFRS 3/IFRS 3

lessons.title: Lesson 11: Impairment of Assets
lessons.description: Accounting for asset impairment under PFRS 36/IAS 36 including recoverable amount calculations

lessons.title: Lesson 12: Financial Liabilities
lessons.description: Accounting for financial liabilities under PFRS 9/IAS 39 including classification and measurement

lessons.title: Lesson 13: Provisions and Contingencies
lessons.description: Accounting for provisions, contingent liabilities, and contingent assets under PFRS 37/IAS 37

lessons.title: Lesson 14: Employee Benefits
lessons.description: Accounting for employee benefits under PFRS 19/IAS 19 including short-term and post-employment benefits

lessons.title: Lesson 15: Equity
lessons.description: Accounting for equity including share capital, retained earnings, and other equity components

resources.title: Statement of Changes in Equity Template

lessons.title: Lesson 16: Revenue from Contracts with Customers
lessons.description: Revenue recognition under PFRS 15/IFRS 15 including the five-step model and contract modifications

lessons.title: Lesson 17: The Effects of Changes in Foreign Exchange Rates
lessons.description: Accounting for foreign currency transactions and translations under PFRS 21/IAS 21

lessons.title: Lesson 18: Income Taxes
lessons.description: Accounting for income taxes under PFRS 12/IAS 12 including current and deferred tax

resources.title: Deferred Tax Liability and Asset Calculation

lessons.title: Lesson 19: Business Combinations
lessons.description: Accounting for business combinations under PFRS 3/IFRS 3 including acquisition method and goodwill

lessons.title: Lesson 20: Consolidated Financial Statements
lessons.description: Preparation of consolidated financial statements under PFRS 10/IAS 27 and PFRS 3/IFRS 3

lessons.title: Lesson 21: Accounting for Government Grants
lessons.description: Accounting for government assistance and disclosure of government assistance under PFRS 20/IAS 20

sections.title: Section 6: Financial Statement Analysis and Interpretation

lessons.title: Lesson 22: Statement of Cash Flows
lessons.description: Preparation and analysis of statement of cash flows under PFRS 7/IAS 7 using direct and indirect methods

lessons.title: Lesson 23: Financial Ratios and Analysis
lessons.description: Analysis of financial statements using liquidity, profitability, leverage, and efficiency ratios

resources.title: Common Size and Trend Analysis Practice

lessons.title: Lesson 24: Ethical Considerations in Financial Reporting
lessons.description: Professional ethics and responsibilities of accountants under the Philippine Code of Ethics for Professional Accountants

files.title: CPA Board Exam FAR Past Papers (Last 5 Years)
Time Management Strategies for Board Exams
